overview: The Batch Translation step creates translation memory entries from text units extracted from a raw document, using an external command-line tool to provide translations. It extracts content into a temporary HTML document, invokes the external translator, then aligns source and translated segments into a Pensieve TM and/or TMX document. Large documents are handled by processing text units in configurable blocks.

command.help:
The command line that invokes your external translation tool. The step creates a temporary HTML file where each paragraph is a source segment, and your tool must read it and produce a translated HTML file in the same format.

Available variables:

| Variable | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `${inputPath}` | Full path of the temp input HTML | |
| `${inputURI}` | URI of the temp input HTML | |
| `${outputPath}` | Full path where the tool must write translated HTML | |
| `${outputURI}` | URI of the expected output | |
| `${srcLangName}` | English name of source language | `de-ch` → `German` |
| `${trgLangName}` | English name of target language | `ja-jp` → `Japanese` |
| `${srcLang}` | Source language code | `de-ch` → `de` |
| `${trgLang}` | Target language code | `ja-jp` → `ja` |
| `${rootDir}` | Project/batch root directory | |
| `${inputRootDir}` | Root directory for input files | |

Locale-related variables are also available.
command.values: A shell command string with `${variable}` placeholders. The external tool **must** read from `${inputPath}` and write translated HTML to `${outputPath}`.
examples: `apertium -f html ${srcLang}-${trgLang} ${inputPath} ${outputPath}` — open-source Apertium MT under Linux

origin.help: An optional identifier string stored as a property on each translated entry. In TMX output, it appears as `<prop type="Txt::Origin">value</prop>`. Useful for tracking which translation tool or run produced the entries.

markAsMT.help: Marks all generated TM entries as machine translation results. When enabled, the `creationId` attribute of the target is set to `"MT!"` in the output. This allows downstream tools and editors to distinguish MT output from human translations.

makeTM.help:
Imports the translated entries into a Pensieve TM at the specified directory. If the TM does not exist, it is created. If it already exists, new entries are appended.

Entries are indexed at the end of each input document, so downstream pipeline steps can only access entries generated from **previous** documents, not the current one.
notes: The TM directory path supports `${rootDir}` and `${inputRootDir}` variables, plus locale variables.

tmDirectory.help: Directory of the Pensieve TM to create or populate. If the directory does not exist, a new TM is created. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables in the path.

makeTMX.help: Creates a TMX document containing all translated entries. A **single** TMX file is generated across all input documents. The file is not written until processing of the last document completes, so it **cannot** be used by downstream pipeline steps.
notes: If the output file already exists, it will be overwritten.

tmxPath.help: Full path of the TMX file to generate. If the file exists, it will be overwritten. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables in the path.

sendTMX.help: Sends the generated TMX document as the raw document to the next pipeline step, **instead of** the original input document. Without this option, the step passes the unmodified input document through.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when makeTMX is enabled, since there is no TMX document to send otherwise

blockSize.help:
Controls how many text units are grouped into each temporary HTML file sent to the external tool. This allows processing of very large documents even when the external tool has size limitations.

For example, a document with 5000 text units and a block size of 1000 will result in 5 separate invocations of the external tool.

checkExistingTm.help:
Looks up each candidate entry in an existing Pensieve TM before sending it for translation. Entries that already have a translation in the existing TM are **skipped** — they are not sent to the external tool and are not included in the optional TMX output.

This avoids re-translating content you already have translations for.

existingTm.help: Directory of the existing Pensieve TM to check for pre-existing translations. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables in the path.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this path to be used

segment.help:
Segments extracted text units using SRX rules before building the temporary HTML. When enabled, each HTML paragraph corresponds to a **sentence**. When disabled, each paragraph is an unsegmented text unit.

Segmentation affects granularity of both the translation and the resulting TM/TMX entries.
notes: Only entries processed by the external tool are placed in the TMX output. Entries that already exist in the TM being populated or in the existing TM are **not** copied into the TMX output.

srxPath.help: Full path to the SRX segmentation rules file. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables in the path.

dependsOn.condition: must be enabled for this path to be used

markAsMT.description: Marks generated TM entries as machine translation by setting creationId to "MT!"

makeTM.description: Imports translated entries into a Pensieve TM, creating it if necessary

makeTMX.description: Creates a TMX document with all translated entries across all input documents

sendTMX.description: Sends the generated TMX document to the next step instead of the original input document

checkExistingTm.description: Skips entries that already have translations in an existing Pensieve TM

segment.description: Segments text units into sentences using SRX rules before translation

processingNotes:
The step extracts content into temporary HTML files where each `

` element is one source segment or text unit. The external tool must preserve this paragraph structure in its output.
Large documents are split into multiple temporary HTML files based on the **block size** setting, with the external tool invoked once per block.
The raw input document passes through the step unmodified (unless **Send TMX to next step** is enabled), so downstream steps see the original document.
Pensieve TM entries are indexed per-document, so downstream steps can only leverage TM entries from previously processed documents in the batch.

examples.title: Apertium MT on Linux
examples.description: Uses the open-source Apertium machine translation system to translate documents. Language pair is derived automatically from the pipeline's source and target locales.
examples.config:
```yaml
command: "apertium -f html ${srcLang}-${trgLang} ${inputPath} ${outputPath}"
blockSize: 1000
markAsMT: true
makeTMX: true
tmxPath: "${rootDir}/output.tmx"
```

examples.title: ProMT on Windows
examples.description: Uses the commercial ProMT application to translate temporary HTML documents on Windows.
examples.config:
```yaml
command: "\"C:\\Program Files\\PRMT9\\FILETRANS\\FileTranslator.exe\" ${inputPath} /as /ac /d:${srcLangName}-${trgLangName} /o:${outputPath}"
blockSize: 500
markAsMT: true
origin: "promt-9"
makeTM: true
tmDirectory: "${rootDir}/tm"
```

fullDoc:
# Batch Translation Step The Batch Translation step creates translation memory entries from text units extracted from a raw document, using an external command-line tool to provide translations. It extracts content into a temporary HTML document, invokes the external translator, then aligns source and translated segments into a Pensieve TM and/or TMX document. Large documents are handled by processing text units in configurable blocks. **Takes:** Raw document. **Sends:** Raw document (or TMX if configured). ## How It Works 1. The step extracts content from the raw document using its associated filter. 2. Extracted segments are written to a temporary HTML file (one `

` per segment). 3. The external tool is invoked via the configured command line — it must read `${inputPath}` and write translated HTML to `${outputPath}`. 4. The step reads back the translated HTML and aligns source/target segments. 5. Aligned entries are stored in a Pensieve TM and/or TMX document. 6. The original raw document is passed to the next step (unless **Send TMX** is enabled). ## Parameters ### Translation #### Command Line The command that invokes your external translation tool. The step creates a temporary HTML file where each paragraph is a source segment. Your tool must read it and produce a translated HTML file in the same format. Available variables: `${inputPath}`, `${inputURI}`, `${outputPath}`, `${outputURI}`, `${srcLang}`, `${trgLang}`, `${srcLangName}`, `${trgLangName}`, `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, plus locale-related variables. #### Block Size Maximum number of text units grouped into each temporary HTML file. Allows processing very large documents with tools that have size limitations. #### Origin Identifier Optional string stored as a property on each translated entry. In TMX output, appears as `value`. #### Mark as Machine Translation Marks generated TM entries as machine translation by setting the `creationId` attribute to `"MT!"`. ### Segmentation #### Segment Text Units Segments extracted text units into sentences using SRX rules before building the temporary HTML. When enabled, each HTML paragraph is a sentence; when disabled, each paragraph is an unsegmented text unit. > **Note:** Only entries processed by the external tool appear in TMX output. Entries found in existing TMs are not copied. #### SRX Rules Path Full path to the SRX segmentation rules file. Supports `${rootDir}`, `${inputRootDir}`, and locale variables. ### Output — Pensieve TM #### Import into Pensieve TM Imports translated entries into a Pensieve TM. If the TM does not exist, it is created; otherwise entries are appended. Entries are indexed per-document, so downstream steps only see entries from previously processed documents. #### Pensieve TM Directory Directory of the Pensieve TM to create or populate. ### Output — TMX #### Create TMX Document Creates a TMX document with all translated entries. A single file is generated across all input documents and is not written until the last document completes. #### TMX Output Path Full path of the TMX file to generate. Overwrites existing files. #### Send TMX to Next Step Sends the generated TMX document as the raw document to the next pipeline step instead of the original input document. ### Existing TM Lookup #### Check Existing TM Looks up each candidate entry in an existing Pensieve TM before translation. Entries with existing translations are skipped — not sent to the external tool and not included in TMX output. #### Existing TM Directory Directory of the existing Pensieve TM to check for pre-existing translations. ## Limitations None known. ## Notes - The temporary HTML preserves paragraph structure — the external tool must maintain the same number and order of paragraphs. - Large documents are split across multiple invocations based on block size. - The raw input document passes through unmodified unless **Send TMX** is enabled. - Pensieve TM indexing is per-document, so downstream steps can only access entries from earlier documents in the batch. ## Examples ### Apertium MT on Linux ```yaml command: "apertium -f html ${srcLang}-${trgLang} ${inputPath} ${outputPath}" blockSize: 1000 markAsMT: true makeTMX: true tmxPath: "${rootDir}/output.tmx" ``` ### ProMT on Windows ```yaml command: "\"C:\\Program Files\\PRMT9\\FILETRANS\\FileTranslator.exe\" ${inputPath} /as /ac /d:${srcLangName}-${trgLangName} /o:${outputPath}" blockSize: 500 markAsMT: true origin: "promt-9" makeTM: true tmDirectory: "${rootDir}/tm" ```
